entries.title: Daily drill: Ray Serve Head Node Preemption — Stale GCS Actor Handles Zombie All GPU Workers
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty ML Serving hotfix drill: Azure AKS spot VM preemption kills the Ray head node, causing GCS epoch rollover. All 8 GPU worker replicas (Mistral-7B on A100-40GB) become zombie actors holding 36.4 GB GPU memory each with 0% utilization, blocking all 14,200 active WebSocket sessions for 22 minutes. Exercise covers epoch-mismatch triage via ray status, immediate mitigation via daemonset rollout restart, and long-term fixes including Redis-backed GCS persistence and IMDS eviction alerting.
items_added: Ray Serve Head Node Preemption — Stale GCS Actor Handles Zombie All GPU Workers

entries.title: Daily drill: Cassandra Tombstone Accumulation Kills Feature Store Reads
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty Cassandra hotfix drill covering tombstone accumulation in an ML feature store table after 9 months of DELETE+INSERT writes. Scenario includes exact ReadTimeoutException messages, nodetool tablehistograms output (p99 tombstones_scanned: 218,432), G1GC pause timings, and a 5-step exercise from compaction hotfix through TWCS migration.
items_added: Cassandra Tombstone Accumulation Kills Feature Store Reads

entries.title: Daily drill: Triton Inference Server Silent Fallback — Model Config/Weight Shape Mismatch
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty ML Serving hotfix drill: Triton silently falls back to stale model weights after a config/weight shape mismatch breaks hot-reload from S3. Scenario includes exact Triton error log lines, SageMaker Feature Store context, GitHub Actions CI bug, and a 6-step exercise from triage to prevention.

entries.title: Daily drill: Kafka Consumer Livelock — max.poll.interval.ms Breach During Downstream Throttle
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty distributed-systems hotfix drill: a Java Kafka consumer group on AWS EKS enters an infinite livelock when a SendGrid API slowdown (48ms → 11.2s) causes batch processing to exceed max.poll.interval.ms=300s. All 6 consumers repeatedly get ejected and rejoin without committing offsets, growing lag at 195K messages/minute across 120 partitions. Exercise covers triage via JMX rebalance-rate metrics, immediate mitigation by reducing max.poll.records, circuit-breaker isolation of the downstream, and prevention via dynamic poll sizing and rebalance-rate alerting.
items_added: Kafka Consumer Livelock — max.poll.interval.ms Breach During Downstream Throttle

entries.title: Daily drill: Kafka Preferred Leader Election Drift After Broker Restart
entries.summary: Added a Medium-difficulty distributed systems hotfix drill covering Kafka partition leader imbalance on AWS MSK after a broker restart, with exact CloudWatch metrics, consumer lag figures, and a 5-step exercise from diagnosis through prevention using preferred leader election.
items_added: Kafka Preferred Leader Election Drift After Broker Restart

entries.title: Daily drill: PostgreSQL Transaction ID Wraparound — Emergency Vacuum Under Active Blocker
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty PostgreSQL hotfix drill: a fintech ledger's transactions table (1.1B rows, 1.8TB) approaches TXID wraparound (~39 minutes to hard shutdown) after an 11.6-hour analytics ETL query blocks autovacuum workers on AWS RDS. Scenario includes exact Postgres WARNING log lines, pg_stat_activity blocker chain, and a 6-step exercise covering pg_terminate_backend emergency, manual VACUUM FREEZE, autovacuum cost throttle bypass, and prevention via idle_in_transaction_session_timeout and relfrozenxid age alerting.
items_added: PostgreSQL Transaction ID Wraparound — Emergency Vacuum Under Active Blocker

entries.title: Daily drill: gRPC Context Leak — Goroutine Accumulation from Unbound Background Workers
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty distributed-systems hotfix drill: a Go 1.22 gRPC ranking service on AWS EKS accumulates 22,400 goroutines (from a baseline of 1,100) over 47 minutes after an ElastiCache Redis failover causes background workers spawned with context.Background() to block for their full 5s socket timeout instead of respecting the client's 500ms deadline. Exercise covers pprof goroutine triage, rolling-restart mitigation, context propagation fix, Redis circuit breaker, and goroutine leak prevention via goleak and staticcheck.
items_added: gRPC Context Leak — Goroutine Accumulation from Unbound Background Workers

entries.title: Daily drill: vLLM Speculative Decoding Draft Model Vocab Mismatch — Corrupted Responses After Target Model Rollout
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty ML Serving hotfix drill on GKE: vLLM 0.4.2 speculative decoding returns HTTP 200 but only 7–9 token truncated responses after a target model upgrade expands the tokenizer vocab from 32K to 36K while the draft model remains at 32K. A vLLM engine off-by-one bug in the spec-decode rejection rollback loop corrupts the output buffer pointer on last-token rejections. Exercise covers spec-decode metric triage (acceptance_rate→0.00), disabling spec-decode as immediate mitigation, upgrading to vLLM 0.4.3, and adding ArgoCD PreSync vocab-parity hooks and synthetic quality canaries.
items_added: vLLM Speculative Decoding Draft Model Vocab Mismatch — Corrupted Responses After Target Model Rollout
